description: Combined benefits comparison for Insurer1 (Plan A) and Insurer2 (Plan B). All values in INR unless stated.
regulatory_basis: IRDAI (Insurance Products) Regulations 2024, Master Circular 29 May 2024

benefits.notes: Plan A offers higher sum insured reflecting a premium-tier plan. Plan B is a mid-tier plan. Both are individual policies.

Plan A aggregate deductible is shared across all claims in a year. Plan B per-claim deductible resets with each new hospitalisation episode. Plan B's structure means higher total patient exposure if multiple claims occur in a year.
After deductible, Plan A insurer covers 80% of admissible amount; Plan B covers 75%. Patient bears 20% and 25% respectively.
Plan A has no co-payment for standard members. Plan B penalises non-network usage with a 10% co-payment on top of the coinsurance share.
Caps total patient liability. Once reached, insurer pays 100% of further admissible claims for the policy year.
Both plans cover ACL reconstruction. 24-month waiting period for pre-existing conditions. Acute trauma covered from day 1.
Both plans cover meniscus repair/removal. Same waiting period and pre-auth rules as ACL.
Both cover post-hospitalisation physiotherapy only. OPD-only PT without linked admission not covered in base plan. Plan A has higher sub-limit and longer post-discharge window.
Diagnostics, consultations, medicines related to the condition leading to admission. Plan A's 60-day window covers MRI and specialist consultations done weeks before surgery.
Follow-up consultations, physiotherapy, medicines after discharge. Plan A's 90-day window is more generous.
Arthroscopic ACL surgery often performed as day care. Both plans cover it.
Both plans offer cashless. Hospital bills sent directly to insurer.
IRDAI mandates 1-hour cashless authorisation for all insurers. Plan B's insurer has committed to 30-minute turnaround as a competitive differentiator.

benefits.planB: 75% of admissible minus 10% copay, room rent capped ₹3,000/day
benefits.notes: Non-network usage is significantly penalised in Plan B due to both lower coinsurance and co-payment.
IRDAI mandates settlement or rejection within 30 days of receiving all documents. 2% above bank rate interest on delays.

benefits.planA: 10% of sum insured per claim-free year, up to 100%
benefits.planB: 5% of sum insured per claim-free year, up to 50%
benefits.notes: Reward for not claiming. Plan A accumulates bonus faster. Bonus increases sum insured without extra premium.
IRDAI 2024 Master Circular mandates lifetime renewal for all health insurance policies. Cannot be refused except for proven fraud.
